artifact_title: Failure Attribution Note: Fluent Model Artifact vs Trace Conflict

claims.text: Failures must be attributed to one of the defined layers: model, protocol, harness, substrate, provider, scorer, or inconclusive.
Prior contamination scoring misclassified faithful invalidations, proving scorer faults are possible.
Audit-grade rows preserve raw request, response, parsed action, accepted/rejected operations, and deterministic scorer output.

conclusion: The failure is attributed to the scorer layer. Prior cases show scorer faults are possible, and the preserved raw trace allows verification of the scorer's output against the model artifact.
recommended_action: Review the raw trace to verify the scorer output and compare it with the model artifact to confirm misclassification.
